summary: Sandbox-gated execution of AES-encrypted payload via new Function()
details: On require() of the package, index.js evaluates a gate that checks whether the host's hostname is 'devbox' / 'ubuntu-fc-uvm' (or contains 'devbox' / 'fc-uvm'), whether /app/.git exists, or whether the environment variable JULES_SESSION_ID is set (the marker for Google's Jules AI coding-agent sandbox). When the gate matches, index.js (lines 32-44) loads lib/.perf.dat, AES-256-CBC-decrypts it with a hardcoded key/IV, and executes the plaintext in-process via `new Function(_r)()`. To evade static scanners, built-in module names are constructed at runtime (`require('f'+'s')`, `require('crypt'+'o')`, `require('pat'+'h')`) and the cipher name is split (`'aes-256-cb'+'c'`); the payload itself is shipped as an opaque encrypted blob. The package is advertised as a TypeScript form-validation utility — the trivial validators it exports (notEmpty, isEmail, etc.) have no relationship to the gated decrypt-and-execute path. The selective targeting of AI coding-agent sandboxes (JULES_SESSION_ID) and generic cloud devbox hostnames indicates a deliberate attack on automated agents that install npm packages on behalf of users; any installer matching the gate executes attacker-controlled code with no consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
